description:
You are a system administrator overseeing two application nodes, “ServerA” and “ServerB”.  
Each node periodically writes to its own local log file, which has already been copied to your workstation:

        /home/user/serverA.log
        /home/user/serverB.log

Both log files use the same format:

        YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS <LEVEL> <MESSAGE>

Your objective is to do a quick health-check for the distributed service by counting how many lines in each log contain the string “ERROR”.  
Summarise the results in a *new* text file called

        /home/user/error_summary.txt

The file must be created (or overwritten if it already exists) and must **contain exactly two lines**, one for each server, in this precise, case-sensitive format (including the single space after the colon):

        ServerA: <number_of_errors> errors
        ServerB: <number_of_errors> errors

For example, if ServerA had 4 errors and ServerB had 5, the file would look like:

        ServerA: 4 errors
        ServerB: 5 errors

Additional requirements to be verified by automated tests:
1. The summary file **must end with a trailing newline** after the second line.
2. No extra spaces, tabs, or blank lines are allowed.
3. After writing the file, display its contents to stdout so the operator sees the same two-line report in the terminal.

Because this is an on-call “triage” task, you are expected to achieve it with a simple one-liner or a very small set of shell commands (roughly 2–3 commands total).  
Do not modify the original log files; only read from them.  
When you are done, the automated grader will look exclusively at:

        /home/user/error_summary.txt
truth:
Initial state (pre-loaded before the agent starts):

File: /home/user/serverA.log  
Contents:
2023-08-01 12:00:01 INFO Service started
2023-08-01 12:10:15 ERROR Connection timeout
2023-08-01 12:11:00 INFO Reconnected
2023-08-01 12:20:05 ERROR Disk full
2023-08-01 12:30:00 WARNING High memory usage

File: /home/user/serverB.log  
Contents:
2023-08-01 12:05:00 INFO Service started
2023-08-01 12:15:45 ERROR Connection refused
2023-08-01 12:16:30 ERROR Connection refused
2023-08-01 12:18:20 INFO Reconnected
2023-08-01 12:25:55 ERROR Disk full

Correct solution that grader will validate:

File: /home/user/error_summary.txt  
Exact byte-for-byte contents (including final newline):
ServerA: 2 errors
ServerB: 3 errors

Note: the file ends with a newline after “errors”.  
Permissions: default user-writable regular file (0644) owned by user.
name: task_000000_0e5b95da
